note: Correctly pristine/sharp with no visible compression artifacts — right for a high-bitrate H.264 Blu-ray. Good restraint (clean is the authentic look here).
Clean, vivid, sharp — correct for modern high-bitrate HEVC/AV1 streaming. Authentic by being near-artifact-free.
Mostly clean with only mild softness — correct for high-bitrate MPEG-2 DVD, and well differentiated from the heavily-blocky low-bitrate web presets.
Shows real DCT blocking + occasional coloured macroblock errors + smear, matching the codec reference's block/mosquito behaviour. Good.
Visible 8x8 block edges on the flat colour blocks plus scattered coloured macroblock errors — authentic XviD low-bitrate behaviour.
Per-frame MJPEG blocking + an authentic 'REC / BAT 84%' camcorder OSD and timestamp. Era-true.
Soft mobile H.264 with mild compression — plausible for a phone livestream. Could add slight motion-blocking on movement.
Soft with mild blocking + a timecode/UNIT OSD. To nail Zoom specifically, add the heavy H.264 macroblocking + temporal smear that appears on motion at low upstream bandwidth.
Soft, mildly compressed with a REC/BAT OSD — plausible early phone video.
Soft and low-res. Add the defining webcam traits: heavy low-light luma/chroma noise, low frame rate smear, and aggressive auto-exposure — currently it reads cleaner than a real 2008 webcam.
Soft low-quality VGA-ish look — correct for the iPhone 3G's notoriously poor video. Plausible.
Clean modern mobile H.264 with an OSD overlay — plausible for a phone story/livestream.
Generational re-compression is well represented — coloured macroblock errors + heavy blocking from repeated re-encodes, the signature of a re-uploaded compilation.
Strong 'deep-fried' re-compression — blocking + coloured corruption + screen-record softness, authentic for a many-times-reposted clip.
Convincing satellite MPEG-2 break-up — scattered coloured macroblock errors and block-freeze/smear concentrated in bands, matching real DVB signal loss.
Real datamosh behaviour — block-wise smear/bloom where P-frame motion is applied to stale content after I-frame removal. A genuine digital phenomenon, correctly blocky rather than a soft blur.
Random coloured macroblock corruption + displacement across the frame — authentic bit-rot/corrupted-bitstream decode errors.

Very soft/smeary, which is partly right for RealVideo, but the codec reference shows hard 8x8 block edges that are mostly absent here — leans gaussian blur over blocky DCT. Add subtle block structure.
MPEG-1 VCD should show COARSE 8x8 blocking on gradients (per the codec reference); currently reads more as soft blur than the chunky MPEG-1 block grid.
Plausible low-quality web video but leans blur; add visible DCT block edges + mosquito ringing around high-contrast edges to match the codec reference.
2007 YouTube used Sorenson/H.263 at low bitrate which blocks heavily; the render is soft rather than blocky. Push the block structure + mosquito noise.
Currently just clean. Early HDSLR video is defined by line-skipping moiré/aliasing on fine detail and rolling-shutter skew — neither is present. Add those rather than generic softness.

STRENGTH: produces genuine block-aligned coloured macroblock errors + displacement/smear matching real codec corruption (per digital-compression-derived). Drives the convincing Satellite/Datamosh/Bit-Rot/Vine/TikTok looks.

On low-bitrate LEGACY presets (RealPlayer/VCD/YouTube2007/Early Web) the compression reads as gaussian softness rather than the hard 8x8 DCT BLOCK boundaries on flat gradients + mosquito ringing around high-contrast edges that the codec reference shows. The block-edge primitive should be visible at low quality, not just blur.
